美国生产的水泥和混凝土的人工智能

AI for American-produced cement and concrete

engineering.fb.com 作者 latchkey 分数 181 评论 109 2026/04/02 01:17
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. Meta 正在继续其长期路线图，帮助建筑行业利用人工智能生产高质量和更可持续的混凝土混合物，以及在美国独家生产的混凝土混合物。在 2026 年美国混凝土协会 (ACI) 春季大会的同时，Meta 发布了用于设计混凝土配合比的新 AI 模型 - 混凝土贝叶斯优化 (BOxCrete)，以及用于开发屡获殊荣的混凝土配合比的基础数据。 Meta 的可持续混凝土开源模型现已在 GitHub 上发布。
  2. 美国每年浇筑约 4 亿立方码混凝土，这些混凝土足以铺设绕地球多次的双车道高速公路。它是我们桥梁、数据中心、高速公路和家庭的支柱。然而，虽然我们大部分预拌混凝土是在国内生产的，但制造它的水泥却有近四分之一是进口的。 Meta 的人工智能正在帮助改变这一现状。混凝土由水泥和胶凝材料、骨料、水和化学外加剂的混合物组成。混凝土供应商必须设计混凝土混合物以满足竞争性要求：强度、速度、易于处理、成本和可持续性。传统的混凝土配合比设计在很大程度上依赖于实验室中的反复试验、工程师的直觉和数十年积累的知识，这是一个缓慢且昂贵的工作流程。水泥是混凝土的关键成分，因此进口水泥可能对美国供应商产生重大影响，抑制美国制造业、就业和投资。虽然预拌混凝土通常在国内生产，但其所需的水泥大量进口，美国水泥消耗量的约 20-25% 由进口满足。此外，美国制造的水泥符合美国的性能和环境标准，但与国际上不一致。与此同时，确保产品在国内生产（这一过程通常称为回流）通常会增加美国的制造业就业机会。自 2020 年以来，回流和相关外国直接投资 (FDI) 已为美国带来了超过 110 万个就业岗位，而制造业是经济乘数最高的行业之一；制造业每花费 1 美元，就会为美国经济增加 2.69 美元。仅水泥和混凝土行业每年就贡献超过 1,300 亿美元，并提供约 60 万个就业岗位，但进口仍满足国内总需求的约 23%。为了在国内获得更多的价值，美国的混凝土生产商希望在其混合物中加入更多美国制造的材料。不同的水泥具有不同的化学成分，与一种水泥完美配合的混合物可能与另一种水泥完全失效。因此，生产商需要一种快速探索和验证新配方的方法，而无需在实验室花费数月时间。 Meta 及其合作伙伴已因混凝土设计方面的这些创新而获得了多项奖项，包括 2025 年最佳合作伙伴建筑创新奖（与 Amrize 共享）和 2025 年可持续混凝土项目矿渣水泥奖（与 Amrize 和伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校共享）。但通过与大型混凝土制造商和软件公司的合作，几个州的实地合作也感受到了这种模式的影响。伊利诺伊州梅塔一直与伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校和总部位于伊利诺伊州芝加哥的北美最大的水泥和混凝土制造商 Amrize 密切合作，在可持续和国产混凝土中实施人工智能。 Amrize 在北美经营着 18 个水泥厂、141 个水泥码头和 269 个预拌混凝土场地。他们的规模使他们成为展示人工智能如何改变工业量混合设计的理想合作伙伴。 Amrize 最近推出了“美国制造”水泥标签，保证水泥符合严格的美国标准，并由美国国内劳动力使用美国材料制造。该公司最近还宣布到 2026 年进行近 10 亿美元的资本投资，部分目的是增加国内水泥产量。 Meta 和 Amrize 将与伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校的研究人员一起出席美国混凝土协会 (ACI) 春季大会，进一步展示我们利用人工智能生产低排放国产混凝土的合作伙伴关系。在此次活动期间，Meta 还发布了用于设计混凝土配合比的新 AI 模型：混凝土贝叶斯优化 (BOxCrete)。 BOxCrete 改进了 Meta 之前的模型，对噪声数据具有更强的鲁棒性，并提供了新功能，包括预测混凝土坍落度（混凝土和易性的重要指标）的能力。 Meta 与 BOxCrete 相结合，发布了用于开发明尼苏达州罗斯蒙特数据中心使用的新型混凝土混合物的基础数据。与其他开源、已发布的数据集相比，该基础数据是混凝土配合比性能的最佳系统基础数据。 Meta 的研究人员已提交了一篇有关 BOxCrete 的论文供发表，其中概述了新模型、数据和相关方法。明尼苏达州与 Amrize、Mortenson 和伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校合作，使用 BOxCrete 生成更坚固、固化速度更快的混凝土混合物，并在我们位于明尼苏达州罗斯蒙特的一个数据中心建筑板的现场支撑部分中大规模使用。 AI 优化的组合专为建筑中要求最高的部分之一：支撑数千台服务器和冷却系统重量的巨大混凝土基础。使用国内采购的材料，混合物达到完全结构强度的速度比原始配方快 43%，同时也将开裂风险降低了近 10%，这证明人工智能可以帮助美国生产商在不牺牲质量的情况下快速重新配制美国制造的材料。数据确认其满足所有结构要求，该混合物现在可以在数据中心的其他区域使用。 Meta 位于明尼苏达州罗斯蒙特的数据中心。宾夕法尼亚州 2023 年，Meta 在 MIT 许可下将其具体优化人工智能框架作为开源软件发布，从而实现了从学术界到商业软件提供商的广泛采用。为了反映人工智能驱动的配合比设计如何成为混凝土生产标准基础设施的一部分，总部位于宾夕法尼亚州的 Quadrel（一家服务于预拌行业的领先企业 SaaS 平台）在其软件中采用了 Meta 的人工智能框架。 Quadrel 已将其应用于实际用例，包括数据预处理、批量和测试标准化、特征工程和客户特定的模型训练。随着现场测试结果的融入，这些模型不断改进，并已嵌入到日常混合设计和质量控制工作流程中，为质量控制和运营中的日常决策提供信息。 Meta 的可持续混凝土开源人工智能模型是在麻省理工学院的许可下提供的，允许以最小的限制进行商业用途，同时受益于开源人工智能的进步和投资。 Meta 如何利用人工智能开发混凝土混合物 Meta 的人工智能混凝土模型可以帮助供应商通过一种称为自适应实验的方法更快地将美国材料融入到他们的混合物中。它的工作原理如下：Meta 的自适应实验 (Ax) 平台使用贝叶斯优化来智能地导航可能的混凝土配方的广阔空间。人工智能不是随机测试混合或仅依赖人类直觉，而是：
  3. 从现有数据中学习：历史混合设计、实验室结果和性能指标对模型进行训练，了解哪些方法有效。提出高潜力候选材料：人工智能建议最有可能满足目标规格的新混合，并可以比较美国制造和外国材料之间的性能。预先纳入约束：用户指定技术要求和要使用的成分。每次测试都进行改进：每个实验室结果都会改进模型的预测，从而形成自动改进循环。
  4. 虽然人工智能和自适应实验的加入不会改变实验室验证、现场试验、工程签核和代码合规性的过程，但它极大地提高了发现速度，帮助工程师以更少的测试找到更好的起点。
  5. 为混凝土打造人工智能辅助的未来 Meta 的混凝土人工智能是应用机器学习的更广泛承诺的一部分，它可以推动可衡量的现实世界影响。虽然与 Amrize、伊利诺伊大学和 Quadrel 等行业软件提供商的合作代表了第一波采用，但目标是在整个行业范围内改变美国生产商进行混合设计的方式。未来几年，Meta 计划与建筑行业进一步合作，开发新的人工智能工具。随着像 Quadrel 这样的更多平台建立在 BOxCrete 上，制作人可以使用人工智能优化的混音设计，而无需更改现有的工作流程。该团队还计划与伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校继续进行学术合作，探索人工智能如何不仅解决国内材料替代问题，而且解决具体可持续性和性能方面更广泛的挑战。通过减少国内材料采用的障碍，Meta 正在帮助美国生产商在成本上竞争、减少排放并建立供应链弹性，一次一种组合。参与进来 在 GitHub 上探索 Meta 的开源 BOxCrete for Sustainable Concrete。阅读我们的预印本：“BOxCrete：用于混凝土强度预测和配合比优化的贝叶斯优化开源人工智能模型。”

Original Summary

  1. Meta is continuing its long-term roadmap to help the construction industry leverage AI to produce high-quality and more sustainable concrete mixes, as well as those exclusively produced in the United States. Concurrent with the 2026 American Concrete Institute (ACI) Spring Convention, Meta is releasing a new AI model for designing concrete mixes – Bayesian Optimization for Concrete (BOxCrete), as well as the foundational data used to develop award-winning concrete mixes. Meta’s open source model for sustainable concrete is available today on GitHub.
  2. Every year, the United States pours roughly 400 million cubic yards of concrete, enough concrete to pave a two-lane highway that circles the Earth multiple times. It’s the backbone of our bridges, data centers, highways, and homes. However, while we produce most of our ready-mix concrete domestically, we import nearly a quarter of the cement that makes it. Meta’s AI is helping change that. Concrete consists of a mix of cement and cementitious materials, aggregates, water, and chemical admixtures. Concrete suppliers have to design concrete mixes to meet competing requirements: strength, speed, ease of handling, cost, and sustainability. Traditional concrete mix design relies heavily on trial-and-error in the lab, engineer intuition, and decades of accumulated knowledge—a workflow that is slow and expensive to adapt. Cement is a key element of concrete, thus imported cement can have a significant impact on U.S. suppliers, stifling U.S. manufacturing, jobs and investments. While ready-mix concrete is typically produced domestically, the cement required for it is heavily imported, with roughly 20-25% of U.S. cement consumption met by imports. Additionally, cement made in the U.S. complies with U.S. performance and environmental standards that are not consistent internationally. At the same time, ensuring products are produced domestically—a process often called reshoring — generally increases manufacturing jobs in the United States. Reshoring and related foreign direct investment (FDI) have brought over 1.1 million jobs back to the U.S. since 2020, and manufacturing has one of the highest economic multipliers; with every $1.00 spent in manufacturing adding $2.69 to the U.S. economy. The cement and concrete sector alone contributes more than $130 billion annually and supports roughly 600,000 jobs — yet imports still supply about 23% of total domestic demand. To capture more of that value at home, U.S.-based concrete producers want to incorporate more U.S.-made materials in their mixes. Different cements have different chemistries, and a mix that works perfectly with one cement might fail entirely with another. As a result, producers need a way to rapidly explore and validate new formulations without spending months in the lab. Real-World Impact Across the U.S. Meta and its partners have already received a number of awards for these innovations in concrete design, including a 2025 Building Innovation Award for Best Partnership (shared with Amrize) and a Slag Cement Award in 2025 for Sustainable Concrete Project of the Year (shared with Amrize and the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign). But the impact of this model is also being felt through on-the-ground collaborations in several states through partnerships with large-scale concrete manufacturers and software companies. Illinois Meta has been partnering closely with the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and Amrize, the largest cement and concrete manufacturer in North America, headquartered in Chicago, IL., on the implementation of AI for sustainable and domestically-produced concrete. Amrize operates 18 cement plants, 141 cement terminals and 269 ready-mix concrete sites across North America. Their scale makes them an ideal partner for demonstrating how AI can transform mix design at industrial volumes. Amrize recently launched a Made in America cement label, which guarantees the cement meets rigorous U.S. standards and was manufactured in the U.S. by a domestic workforce with American materials. The company also recently announced close to $1 billion of capital investments in 2026 in part to increase domestic cement production. Meta and Amrize will be presenting at the American Concrete Institute (ACI) Spring Convention, along with researchers from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign to further showcase our partnership leveraging AI for lower-emission, domestically-produced concrete. Alongside the event, Meta is releasing a new AI model for designing concrete mixes, Bayesian Optimization for Concrete (BOxCrete). BOxCrete improves over Meta’s previous models with more robustness to noisy data as well as new features including the ability to predict concrete slump (an important indicator of concrete workability). Coupled with BOxCrete, Meta is releasing the foundational data used to develop the novel concrete mix used in our Rosemount, MN data center. This foundational data is the best systematic foundational data for concrete mix performance compared to other open-sourced, published datasets. Meta’s researchers have submitted a paper on BOxCrete for publication that outlines the new model, data, and the associated methodology. Minnesota In partnership with Amrize, Mortenson and the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, BOxCrete was used to generate a stronger, faster-curing concrete mix that was used at scale in a site support section in one of our data center building slabs in Rosemount, MN. The AI-optimized mix was designed for one of the most demanding parts of the build: the massive concrete foundation that supports the weight of thousands of servers and cooling systems. Using domestically sourced materials, the mix reached full structural strength 43% faster than the original formula, while also reducing cracking risk by nearly 10% — proving that AI can help American producers rapidly reformulate around U.S.-made materials without sacrificing quality. With the data confirming it meets all structural requirements, the mix is now qualified for use in additional areas of the data center. Meta’s data center in Rosemount, MN. Pennsylvania In 2023, Meta released its concrete optimization AI framework as open-source software under the MIT license, enabling broad adoption from academia to commercial software providers. In an effort that reflects how AI-driven mix design is becoming part of the standard infrastructure of concrete production, Pennsylvania-based Quadrel, a leading enterprise SaaS platform serving the ready-mix industry, has adapted Meta’s AI framework in its software. Quadrel has applied it to real-world use cases including data preprocessing, batch and test normalization, feature engineering, and customer-specific model training. The models, which continuously improve over time as field test results are incorporated, have been embedded into daily mix design and quality control workflows, informing day-to-day decisions in quality control and operations. Meta’s open-source AI model for sustainable concrete is provided under MIT license, allowing for commercial use with minimum restrictions while benefiting from open-source AI advances and investments. How Meta Leverages AI for Concrete Mixtures Meta’s AI for concrete model can help suppliers more quickly incorporate U.S. materials into their mixes through an approach called adaptive experimentation. Here’s how it works: Meta’s Adaptive Experimentation (Ax) platform uses Bayesian optimization to intelligently navigate the vast space of possible concrete formulations. Instead of testing mixes randomly or relying solely on human intuition, the AI:
  3. Learns from existing data: Historical mix designs, lab results, and performance metrics train the model on what works Proposes high-potential candidates: The AI suggests new mixes most likely to meet target specifications and can compare performance between U.S.-made and foreign materials Incorporates constraints upfront: Users specify technical requirements and the ingredients to be used. Refines with each test: Every lab result improves the model’s predictions, giving rise to an automatic improvement loop.
  4. While the inclusion of AI and adaptive experimentation does not change the process of lab validation, field trials, engineering sign-off, and code compliance, it greatly improves the speed of discovery, helping engineers find better starting points with fewer tests.
  5. Building an AI-Assisted Future for Concrete Meta’s AI for concrete is part of a broader commitment to applying machine learning where it can drive measurable, real-world impact. While the work with Amrize, the University of Illinois, and industry software providers like Quadrel represents the first wave of adoption, the goal is an industry-wide shift in how American producers approach mix design. Over the next few years, Meta is planning to further collaborate with the construction industry to develop new AI tools. As more platforms like Quadrel build on BOxCrete, AI-optimized mix design becomes accessible to producers without requiring them to change their existing workflows. The team is also planning on continued academic collaboration with the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign to explore how AI can address not just domestic material substitution, but broader challenges in concrete sustainability and performance. By reducing the barriers to domestic material adoption, Meta is helping American producers compete on cost, reduce emissions, and build supply chain resilience, one mix at a time. Get Involved Explore Meta’s open-source BOxCrete for Sustainable Concrete on GitHub. Read our pre-print: “BOxCrete: A Bayesian Optimization Open-Source AI Model for Concrete Strength Forecasting and Mix Optimization.”

评论（默认中文）

Animats 2026/04/02 02:10
用于测试混凝土性能的手持设备会更有用。大多数混凝土问题都源于混合不良——水太多、水泥不足等。混凝土测试通常包括从浇筑的板中切出一个核心并将其送到实验室。在混合物中插入探针并在倾倒前将其剔除的东西会有所帮助。以下是一些现场混凝土测试仪。[1]它们很重并且使用起来很痛苦。

应该有一个应用程序可以做到这一点。但这是过去十年的事了。

[1] https://store.forneyonline.com/concrete-testing-equipment/fr...

Hand-held devices for testing concrete properties would be more useful. Most concrete problems come from a bad mix - too much water, not enough cement, etc. Concrete testing usually involves cutting a core out of the poured slab and sending it to a lab. Something where you stick a probe in the mix and can reject it before pouring would help. Here are some on-site concrete testers.[1] They're heavy and a pain to use.

There should be an app for this. But that's so last-decade.

[1] https://store.forneyonline.com/concrete-testing-equipment/fr...

GorbachevyChase 2026/04/02 05:20
通常在这些评论前加上凭据作为前缀，所以我只会说我是一名道路工程师。在批量工厂甚至卡车上取样并不能告诉您全部情况。虽然承包商最常见的犯罪行为是对混凝土浆料过度浇水以使其具有流动性，但由于未能振动混凝土形式、留下空隙或振动过多，导致骨料离析，可能会出现其他工艺问题。如果修整者过度加工混凝土或试图在混凝土生坯时纠正形状，则可能会损害强度。如果混凝土过早完成，可能会出现分层。如果时间允许并且您的承包商很小心，他们可能会保护新浇筑的混凝土并使其湿固化。这使得破解有很大的不同。还有大量的化学添加剂，结构工程师在设计过程中甚至没有考虑到。

我并不是想说混合不重要，或者我不高兴看到 Facebook 正在做一些比监控技术更高尚的事情，但正如许多建设问题一样，事情并没有那么简单。

It’s customary to prefix these comments with credentials, so I’ll just say that I’m a roadway engineer. Sampling at the batch plant or even at the truck is not going to give you the whole story. While the most common crime of contractors is to overwater the concrete slurry to make it flowable, other problems in workmanship can arise from failing to vibrate the concrete in its forms, leaving voids, or vibrating it too much, creating segregation of the aggregate. If the finisher overworks the concrete or tries to correct the shape when it’s green, the that can compromise strength. If the concrete is finished too early, you can get delamination. If time allows and your contractor is careful they might protect the freshly poured concrete and let it cure wet. That makes a huge difference in cracking. There is also a whole world of chemical additives that structural engineers don’t even think about in the design process.

I’m not trying to say that mix doesn’t matter, or that I’m not pleased to see that Facebook is doing something a little more noble than surveillance technology, but as with a lot of construction issues, it’s just not that simple.

datsci_est_2015 2026/04/02 05:26
“事情没那么简单”——我在工业数据科学领域的职业生涯简而言之。许多大公司带着宏大的承诺进入我工作过的领域，虽然有时他们在行业内的高管领导层认为可能的方面取得了进展，但他们也经常给我们提供更层次和更具体（呵呵）产品的小公司带来毒害。例如，诅咒IBM。
“it’s just not that simple” - my career in industrial data science in a nutshell. Lots of large companies come into the domains I’ve worked in with grand promises, and while sometimes they move the needle in terms of what executive leadership within the industry believes is possible, they also often poison the well for us smaller firms who provide much more leveled and concrete (heh) offerings. Curse IBM, for example.

mschuster91 2026/04/02 05:57
人工智能也是一样。世界各地的首席执行官都陷入了人工智能骗子的诱惑，相信他们现在可以集体解雇员工，然后与剩下的员工一起度过难关，直到人工智能赶上剩余 20% 的能力。

然而，专注于人工智能/机器学习的小型公司不从事法学硕士业务吗？即使产品明显有效，他们也很难找到客户，因为他们用错了流行语。

AI is just the same. CEOs all over the world have fallen to the lure of the AI grifters, believing they can fire people en masse now and ride it out with the remaining staff until AI catches up with the remaining 20% of capability.

Small focused AI/ML companies however that don't deal in LLMs? They struggle to find customers, even with stuff that clearly works, because they hit the wrong buzzwords.

munk-a 2026/04/02 03:37
> 水太多、水泥不够等等。

我想说的是，混凝土比人们想象的要复杂得多，地域性也大得多。 砾石和沙子的质量、当地的杂质——这些都起着巨大的作用。 最好将其视为葡萄酒的风土——只不过，与一瓶葡萄酒不同，将成分和最终混合物运送到不同地区的成本过高。 如果一个地区的石灰石含有大量粘土杂质，那么它可能根本不适合大型结构，或者需要大量过滤，以至于不经济。

重要的是要了解当地的地质混合物对混凝土建筑的可行性有多大影响，因为虽然理论上我们可以为每个项目使用完美的混凝土，但在这一点上，大多数项目都太昂贵而无法考虑实施。 有一个非常大的工程领域围绕着确定以你能负担得起的价格所需要的现实性来解决问题。这绝对意味着在费城建造高层建筑所需的材料的价格可能与在米兰规划的相同结构截然不同，即使调整了劳动力对定价的影响。

> too much water, not enough cement, etc.

I wanted to mention that Concrete is far more complex and regional than folks might imagine. The quality of gravel and sand, local impurities - these all contribute massively. It's probably best to think of it like a wine's terroir - except, unlike a bottle of wine, it's prohibitively expensive to ship both the components and the finalized mixture to different areas. If a region's limestone has a massive clay impurity then it may simply be unsuitable for large structures or require extensive filtering to the point of being uneconomical.

It's important to be aware of just how much the local geological mix can impact the viability of building with concrete because while theoretically we could use perfect concrete for every project - at that point most projects would simply be too expensive to consider undertaking. There is a very large field of engineering around establishing the realism required in settling for what you've got for the price you can afford in. It can absolutely mean that the materials required to build a high rise in Philly might be priced starkly differently from the same structure planned in Milan even with adjustments for the labor impact on pricing.

在 HN 中查看
bluGill 2026/04/02 04:27
> 将组件和最终混合物运送到不同地区的成本极其昂贵。

如果重要的话我们可以这样做。 威斯康星州有一些矿场向中东出口沙子，因为众所周知，这种沙子非常适合水力压裂，他们不想冒当地沙子无法正常使用的风险。 （据我所知，他们从未测试过当地的沙子特性，但有可能他们已经测试过，但不起作用）。 在这种情况下，“完美”的价值完全值得高昂的运输成本。

> it's prohibitively expensive to ship both the components and the finalized mixture to different areas.

We could do this if it is important. There are mines in Wisconsin the export sand to the middle east because that is known to work well for fracking and they don't want to risk a local sand not working well. (AFAIK they have never tested local sand properties, but it is possible they have and it doesn't work). In this case the value of the "perfect" is well worth the high shipping costs.

在 HN 中查看
munk-a 2026/04/02 04:44
我们当然可以——这绝对有可能。 问题是它是否经济，而到目前为止，市场在大多数情况下都判定它不经济。 要么该项目不需要如此完美的材料组合（也许存在预期的折旧，但不能证明这样的支出是合理的——可能地震风险会最大限度地减少任何预期的寿命增益——可能材料承包商无法在内部证明增加的材料成本是合理的，同时仍然对当地承包商有吸引力）。

这都是一个平衡。 想象一下这样一个场景，您可以运送专门的材料来建造一座预计寿命为 100 年的桥梁，其成本将达到 5000 万美元，或者您可以使用预期寿命为 15 年的当地混凝土，而材料将花费 500 万美元。 这是对数学的极大简化，但假设这些预期成本，使用当地材料建造并每 15 年更换一次会更便宜。 当然，如果你建造了这座 50M 的桥梁，然后在两年内遭受了一场巨大的海啸，无论如何都摧毁了地基，那么你的脸上就会有鸡蛋。

套用格雷迪的名言：“工程学不是研究建造最好的东西，而是优化我们在成本支出下可以获得的质量。”

We certainly could - it's absolutely possible. The question is if it's economical and so far the market has ruled in most cases that it isn't. Either the project doesn't need such a perfect amalgamation of materials (maybe there is an expected deprecation that doesn't justify such an outlay - possibly earthquake risk would minimize any expected lifespan gain - possibly the materials contractor just can't internally justify the added material cost while remaining attractive to local contractors).

It's all a balance. Imagine a scenario where you can ship in specialized materials to build a bridge with an expected lifespan of 100 years and it'll cost 50M - or you could use local concrete that has an expected lifespan of 15 years and materials would cost 5M. This is a vast simplification of the math but, assuming those expected costs it'd be cheaper to build using local materials and just schedule replacement every 15 years. And, of course, there'll be egg on your face if you build the 50M bridge and then suffer a massive tsunami in two years that destroys the foundations anyways.

To paraphrase a Grady quote: "Engineering isn't a study of building the best thing - it's optimizing the quality we can get for the cost outlay."

在 HN 中查看
cyanydeez 2026/04/02 06:50
对于水力压裂，他们想要的是尺寸完全均匀的圆形石英颗粒。你会发现这种沙子发生了多种过程，特别是冰川地质。您需要均匀的颗粒，因为水力压裂的目标是打开，然后支撑多孔结构，然后可以在不堵塞的情况下抽出液体/气体。

测试此属性并不难，但当您在一个地方发现大量该属性时，成本效率非常显着。它仍然可以被清洗以去除更多的淤泥/粘土，但他们绝对知道该产品的工作原理，并且他们通常知道其他地方的地质情况不会产生相同的材料。

有相当多的材料尺寸，大部分是“我们发现了这种地质材料，这是一些神奇的狗屎”，而不是一些完全制造的人类努力。

for fracking, what they want is a perfectly uniform sized quartz grain thats rounded. You find this sand where multiple processes occur, notably glacial geology. You want uniform grains because frackings goal is to open, then support an porous structure when you can then pull the fluids/gas out without clogging.

It's not really hard to test for this property, but the cost efficiency is notable when you find a massive amount of it in one place. It still may be washed to remove further silt/clay, but they absolutely know the product works and they generally know the geology in other places don't tend to produce the same material.

There's a fair amount of materials size thats mostly "we found this geologic material and this is some magic shit" rather than some wholly manufactured human endevour.

在 HN 中查看
mschuster91 2026/04/02 06:14
重要的不仅仅是砾石/沙子。这 水 其本身的化学成分也有所不同（例如盐、矿物质、碱性/酸性 pH 值），这可能会让您在制造混凝土时变得非常脏。或者在处理水问题时，向弗林特及其臭名昭著的水危机大声疾呼 十一年 为了解决这一问题，必须将每根铅服务管道挖出来并更换。

[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flint_water_crisis#2025

And it's not just the gravel/sand that's important. The water itself also differs in its chemical composition (e.g. salts, minerals, basic/acidic pH), which can catch you really dirty when making concrete. Or when dealing with water at all, shout-out to Flint and its infamous water crisis that took eleven years to resolve - every single lead service pipe had to be physically dug up and replaced.

[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flint_water_crisis#2025

MisterTea 2026/04/02 02:43
在现场，浇注前，他们使用坍落度测试： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Concrete_slump_test
On-site, before pouring, they use the slump test: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Concrete_slump_test
在 HN 中查看
sidewndr46 2026/04/02 02:54
很高兴看到有人指出这一点。测试由一个桶、胶合板和一个秒表组成。
Glad to see someone pointed this out. The test consists of a bucket, plywood board & a stopwatch.
在 HN 中查看
givemeethekeys 2026/04/02 04:28
看起来很简单。有效果吗？
Seems simple. Is it effective?
在 HN 中查看
somerandomqaguy 2026/04/02 05:45
因此，从我记事起，坍落度测试就一直在使用，这是合理的。对于新拌混凝土还需要进行一些其他测试。第一个是混凝土中的空气含量，然后是温度和体积重量。

IIRC 进行的大型测试是从混凝土中取出圆柱体样品，并使其固化至完全强度，然后在浇注后一周到一个月的任何地方进行破坏性测试。

Reasonable so, slump tests have been in use for as long as I can remember. There's a couple of other tests that need to be performed as well for fresh concrete. One for air content in the concrete, then temperature and volumetric weight.

IIRC the big tests that occur are the cylinder samples that are taken as well of the concrete and allowed to cure to full strength before destructively tested anywhere from a week to a month after pouring.

jauntywundrkind 2026/04/02 03:13
希望这里有良好的经验数据为模型提供动力，该模型刚刚添加了衰退预测：

> 在此次活动期间，Meta 还发布了用于设计混凝土配合比的新 AI 模型：混凝土贝叶斯优化 (BOxCrete)。 BOxCrete 改进了 Meta 之前的模型，对噪声数据具有更强的鲁棒性，并提供了新功能，包括预测混凝土坍落度（混凝土和易性的重要指标）的能力。

似乎很难想象，当涉及到你为如此重要的事情进行数百万美元的建设时，不进行衰退测试，相信人工智能。但也许对于作为起点进行规划仍然有用？

Hopefully there's good empirical data powering the model here, which just added slump prediction:

> Alongside the event, Meta is releasing a new AI model for designing concrete mixes, Bayesian Optimization for Concrete (BOxCrete). BOxCrete improves over Meta’s previous models with more robustness to noisy data as well as new features including the ability to predict concrete slump (an important indicator of concrete workability).

Seems hard to imagine not doing a slump test, trusting AI when it comes to your multi/many million dollar build outs for something so important. But perhaps still useful for planning, as a starting place?

在 HN 中查看
bluGill 2026/04/02 04:34
经过坍落度测试的所有不同浇注所产生的废弃混凝土会产生大量费用。混凝土的成本很高，离开工厂后却无法通过坍落度测试——你不仅必须在其他地方清空卡车，而且如果你不能让工人忙起来，可能还会有合同条款。通常会进行不止一项测试 - 如果工厂有一份订单，他们知道要进行测试，他们会在离开工厂之前进行测试（如果失败，他们可以重定向到另一位客户，该客户会故意接受较少的混凝土 - 但混凝土在卡车上固化，因此如果在现场被拒绝并运往其他地方，这是不可接受的）。 许多较小的工作都会跳过测试，但如果免费的话他们希望能完成。

也就是说，我不确定该值是否可以大于浇注前的坍落度测试。

There is a lot of expense in the wasted concrete from all the different pours that are slump tested. There is a lot of cost from concrete that leaves the plant only to fail slump testing when it gets there - not only do you have to empty the truck someplace else, but there maybe contract provisions if you fail to keep the workers busy. Often more than one test is done - if the plant has an order that they know will be tested they test before it leaves the plant (if it fails they can redirect to a different customer who will knowingly accept lesser concrete - but concrete cures on the truck so it would be unacceptable if rejected at the site to go elsewhere). Many smaller jobs skip the test, but they would like it done if it was free.

That said, I'm not sure if the value can ever be greater than a slump test just before pouring.

sebastianeament 2026/04/02 03:16
该模型的预测用作现场测试的建议，以加速寻找具有最佳强度-速度-可持续性权衡的混合物。我们不会用模型取代规范测试。
The predictions of the model are used as recommendations for onsite testing to accelerate finding mixtures with optimal strength-speed-sustainability trade-offs. We are not replacing canonical testing with the model.

Aurornis 2026/04/02 02:24
处理大量在移动时会变干的材料是很困难的。从搅拌混凝土到最终浇筑，有很多步骤。

切下一块板并将其发送到实验室，用于严格施工中的浇注后验证。根据您正在建造的项目的严重程度，有一些预浇注测试要简单得多。

坍落度测试相当简单，例如： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Concrete_slump_test

它基本上是一个带有手柄的锥体，并且过程很容易学习。

Working with multiple tons of material that dries out as you move it around is hard. There are a lot of steps between the concrete being mixed and when it finally reaches the pour.

Cutting out a piece of a slab and sending it to a lab is for post-pour validation in serious construction. There are pre-pour tests that are much simpler depending on the seriousness of what you’re building.

The slump test is rather simple, for example: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Concrete_slump_test

It’s basically a cone with handles and a procedure that’s easy to learn.

在 HN 中查看
munk-a 2026/04/02 03:43
不，这实际上是材料的本地差异，加上在市场之间经济地运输材料的困难。 有些地区有更适合的石灰石、砾石或沙子，并且有能力建造弹性结构，而成本只是其他地区的一小部分。

这里的问题主要是，以经济的方式运输所需数量的混凝土的所有组件非常昂贵。 世界上的一些地区在拥有弹性建筑材料方面刚刚失去了机会。

腐败绝对也是一个问题 - 我无意淡化它 - 但即使我们将其作为一个因素消除，在制造可靠的混凝土过程中也会涉及很多变量......找到良好的混合物是一种艺术形式，例如，如果您的石灰石采石场突然遇到了更多粘土混合体，那么在某些条件下可靠地持续三十年的混凝土可能会突然失去十年的预期寿命。 材料质量的变化也很难检测到，因此除了腐败和偷工减料之外，混凝土混合物还存在真正的质量保证问题。

No - it's actually local variance in materials coupled with the difficulty in moving materials between markets economically. Some areas just have better suited limestone or gravel or sand and can afford to build resilient structures for a fraction of the price that it'd cost in other areas.

This issue here is mainly that it's very expensive to ship all the components of a Concrete in the volume necessary in an economical manner. Some areas of the world just lost the lottery when it comes to having resilient building materials.

Corruption absolutely is an issue as well - I don't mean to downplay it - but even if we remove it as a factor there are just a lot of variables involved in making a reliable Concrete... finding a good mix is an artform and if, for instance, your limestone quary suddenly hits a more clay-laden amalgamation then your Concrete that was reliably lasting for three decades under certain conditions might suddenly lose a decade off the expected lifetime. That change in material quality can also be difficult to detect so there are real quality assurance issues in Concrete mixtures outside of just corruption and cutting corners.

在 HN 中查看
m4rkuskk 2026/04/02 02:42
它们针对给定的组合进行了标准化。在浇注任何东西之前，将基于试用徽章的混合设计提交给 SEOR。配合比设计显示了成分的比例（胶凝材料、细骨料和粗骨料、水、空气、外加剂）。但混凝土仍然是一种非均质材料，有很多变化。以骨料为例，如果过去两周下雨，水分含量会更高，但可能只是该堆上的一层。级配（岩石的粒度）也是如此。有时你会得到一批较小的岩石。有 100 个因素可能会导致泥浆变质。

但是，是的，有些混凝土工厂会偷工减料，试图节省水泥（混合物中最昂贵的部分），这取决于项目，当他们必须付费修理它时，这可能会咬他们的屁股。

They are standardized for a given mix. A mix design that is based on a trial badge is submitted to the SEOR prior to pouring anything. The mix design shows the ratios ingredients (cementitious materials, find and coarse aggregates, water, air, admixtures). But Concrete is still a non-homogeneous material with lots of variations. Take for instance aggregates, if it rained the last two weeks, the moisture content will be higher but it may only be a layer on that pile. Same goes for gradation (particle size of the rock). Sometimes you get a batch with smaller rock. There are a 100 things that can go wrong to get bad mud.

But yeah, there are concrete plants that cut corners and try to save on cement (the most expensive part of the mix), which depending on the project may bite them in the ass when they have to pay to fixing it.

knicholes 2026/04/02 03:20
哎呀，多么轻率的评论。 混合成分（meta 的人工智能正在帮助解决这个问题）与湿混凝土产品是分开的。 家长建议一种在浇注前测试混合物是否正确混合的方法，而不是建议建筑工人在现场确定混合物的化学性质是否正确的方法。 此外，他们甚至没有使用法学硕士，所以它不是“AGI”。

The parent is suggesting a way to test that the mix is properly mixed before pouring, not suggesting a way for construction workers to determine that the chemical properties of the mix will be correct on site. Furthermore, they're not even using LLMs, so it's not "AGI".

wxw 2026/04/02 01:40
惊人的。人们认为混凝土是理所当然的。即使在小规模（例如您的露台）上使用水泥袋上提供的配方，混凝土也可能会出现问题（开裂、结垢、裂缝）。作品中有很多未被欣赏的工艺，不仅在构图和混合方面，这就是这项研究似乎致力于的内容，而且在放置、流平、固化、整理方面。
Awesome. People take concrete for granted. Even at small scales (e.g. your patio) with formulas provided on the cement bag, concrete can go wrong (crazing, scaling, cracks). There's a lot of unappreciated craft in the work, not only in the composition and mixing, which is what this research seems dedicated to, but also in the placing, leveling, curing, finishing.

alephnerd 2026/04/02 01:44
^ 这个。

土木工程是困难的，而混凝土是一个完美的例子，它说明了现实中像混凝土这样“简单”的东西如何需要与化学工程、MatSE、物理、应用数学和计算机科学领域的专家进行大量的跨学科合作。

我在读本科时看到的一些最强大的 HPC 应用程序是由 ONG 领域的土木和结构工程师完成的。

^ This.

Civil Engineering is hard, and concrete is a perfect example of how something as "simple" as concrete in reality requires significant interdisciplinary collaboration with domain experts in ChemE, MatSE, Physics, Applied Math, and CS.

Some of the most robust HPC applications I saw back when I was an undergrad were done by Civil and Structural Engineers in the ONG space.

kevin_thibedeau 2026/04/02 01:45
> 因此，生产商需要一种快速探索和验证新配方的方法，而无需在实验室花费数月时间。

如何绕过浇注测试物品并在固化后数月和数年进行测试的正常过程？从根本上来说，这是一项需要进行可验证的科学的研究活动。法学硕士不是你可以猜测的。

> As a result, producers need a way to rapidly explore and validate new formulations without spending months in the lab.

How do you bypass the normal process of pouring test articles and testing them months and years after cure? This is fundamentally a research activity that needs to conduct verifiable science. Not something you can guess at with an LLM.

在 HN 中查看
sebastianeament 2026/04/02 02:02
你好，我开发了这个模型。我们并没有绕过常规测试过程，也没有使用法学硕士，而是使用经过审查的测试数据的高斯过程。这些预测被用作现场测试的建议，以加速寻找具有最佳强度-速度-可持续性权衡的混合物。
Hi, I developed the model. We are not bypassing the regular testing process, and are not using LLMs, but Gaussian processes with vetted test data. The predictions are used as recommendations for onsite testing, to accelerate finding mixtures with optimal strength-speed-sustainability trade-offs.

That’s helpful. So instead of a much larger test matrix you are using a model to reduce that to the most likely candidates, right?

woah 2026/04/02 02:10
有人需要为当今在线评论中普遍存在的漫无目的的零思考人工智能抱怨创造一个新术语。肉泥？

显然，对于制造商来说，对 100 种可能的候选材料（而不是 100 种随机混合物）进行长达数年的固化测试会更有效率。显然，他们已经通过传统方法筛选候选人，但如果这种人工智能技术能够提高准确性，那就更好了。

Somebody needs to coin a new term for the scattershot zero-thought AI griping that is pervasive in online comments these days. Meatslop?

Obviously it's going to be more productive for a manufacturer to do a years-long curing test on 100 likely candidates instead of 100 random mixes. They obviously already screen candidates through traditional methods, but if this AI technique improves accuracy, all the better.

在 HN 中查看
romaniv 2026/04/02 03:07
人工智能炒作机器目前的策略是通过不断提出难以验证的“积极”主张来耗尽人们的注意力。这是吉什·盖洛普（Gish Gallop）在互联网规模上所做的，由技术影响者、代理“记者”和低价值账户组成的永无休止的游行。整个策略的目标是饱和和士气低落的接受。

毫不奇怪，人们会通过表达对人工智能相关事物的敌意和怀疑来调整他们的直接反应，而不花太多时间进行分析。事实上，这是一个完全理性的回答。

在不了解大局的情况下抱怨它是不诚实的。

在这种特殊情况下，使用“机器学习”一词可能会避免立即的负面反应。

The current strategy of the AI hype machine is to exhaust people's reserves of attention by presenting a never-ending stream of hard-to-verify "positive" claims. It's Gish Gallop done on the Internet scale with a never-ending parade of tech influencers, proxy "journalists" and low-value accounts. The whole strategy aims for saturation and demoralized acceptance.

It's no surprise that people readjust their immediate reactions by expressing hostility and skepticism about anything AI-related without spending much time on analysis. In fact, it's an entirely rational repones.

Complaining about it without acknowledging the larger picture is disingenuous.

In this particular case, using the term "machine learning" would likely avoid the immediate negative reaction.

在 HN 中查看
ainch 2026/04/02 09:11
支持这项工作的高斯过程很难说是“人工智能炒作机器”的产物——它们已经存在了几十年，具有强大的统计基础，并且正在被广泛探索用于跨多个学科的实验设计。对任何种类的机器学习的反射性和信息匮乏的抵制并不比盲目炒作法学硕士更有成效。
The Gaussian Processes underpinning this work are hardly a product of the 'AI Hype Machine' - they've been around for decades, have strong statistical underpinnings, and are being widely explored for experimental design across many disciplines. Reflexive and poorly-informed backlash to any variety of machine learning is no more productive than blindly hyping up LLMs.

rootusrootus 2026/04/02 04:09
Reddit 的顶级贡献者很不错，但有一小部分人（当然，大多数是技术类的）定期出现在 HN 上，但在 Reddit 上贡献不多。

当然，HN 的审核是出于善意，并且比 Reddit 以往任何时候都更普遍地致力于自我审核（除了一些非常小众的 Reddit 子版块），这确实有帮助。

Reddit's top contributors are decent, but there is an elite niche of people (granted, mostly of the technical variety) who somewhat regularly show up on HN but do not contribute much on Reddit.

It does help, of course, that HN is moderated in good faith and has a more pervasive commitment to self-moderation than Reddit has ever had (outside a few very niche subreddits).

ortusdux 2026/04/02 01:53
切线相关，但有新一代的卡车可以在现场搅拌混凝土。 他们可以小批量生产并即时改变混合物。 他们解决了很多令人头疼的问题！

https://cementech.com/volumetric-technology/

Tangentially related, but there is a new generation of trucks that mix the concrete on-site. They can output small batches and change the mix on the fly. They solve a lot of headaches!

https://cementech.com/volumetric-technology/

在 HN 中查看
m4rkuskk 2026/04/02 02:58
这可能适用于小规模，但不适用于大多数商业用例。典型的甲板浇注 (400cy) 将以 70-80cy/小时的速度浇注。你有 9-10cy/卡车。这意味着您有 7 到 8 分钟的时间返回卡车，将其倒入料斗中然后离开。您几乎没有时间在混合物中加水。 大多数大容量混凝土厂都是“干配料”的，这意味着所有配料都被倒入滚筒中，混凝土将在开车到项目现场的过程中进行混合。此外，动态改变混音不会“飞”。没有人会授权进行调整，因为当混合不符合规格时会发生什么……它将需要被削掉。
This may work on a small scale, not in most commercial use cases. A typical deck pour (400cy) will pour at 70-80cy/hr. you got 9-10cy/truck. Meaning you have 7 to 8 minutes to back in the truck, empty it into the hopper and leave. You barely have time to add water to the mix. Most high-volume concrete plants are "dry-batch", which means all the ingredients get dumped into the drum and the concrete will get mixed while driving to the project site. Also, changing mixes on the fly will not "fly". No one is going to authorize the adjustment, because what happens when the mix doesn't meet specs... It will need to get chipped out.
在 HN 中查看
ortusdux 2026/04/02 03:20
这些卡车的目标受众是 10 岁以下的工作岗位。 它允许公司以溢价迎合较小的客户。
The target audience of these trucks is sub-10cy jobs. It allows companies to cater to smaller customers at a premium.

richwater 2026/04/02 02:49
传统的卡车从设施中拾取水泥并旋转它以防止其凝固。他们不会即时混合。任何多余的部分都被视为浪费。
Traditional trucks pick up cement from a facility and rotate it to keep it from setting. They don't mix it on the fly. Any extra is considered waste is poured out.
在 HN 中查看
coryrc 2026/04/02 04:21
您从哪里得到旋转是“防止其凝固”而不是混合的想法？
Where did you get the idea that rotating is "to keep it from setting" and not mixing?
在 HN 中查看
ortusdux 2026/04/02 05:38
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Concrete_mixer#Concrete_mixing...

“专用混凝土运输卡车（在途搅拌机）用于搅拌混凝土并将其运输到施工现场。它们可以装入干燥材料和水，在运输过程中进行混合。它们也可以从“中央混合”工厂装载；通过此过程，材料在装载前已经混合。混凝土搅拌运输卡车通过搅拌或转动滚筒来保持材料的液态，直到交货。”

你们都对。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Concrete_mixer#Concrete_mixing...

"Special concrete transport trucks (in-transit mixers) are made to mix concrete and transport it to the construction site. They can be charged with dry materials and water, with the mixing occurring during transport. They can also be loaded from a "central mix" plant; with this process the material has already been mixed prior to loading. The concrete mixing transport truck maintains the material's liquid state through agitation, or turning of the drum, until delivery. "

Y'all both right.

在 HN 中查看
barbazoo 2026/04/02 01:40
> Meta 的人工智能混凝土模型可以帮助供应商通过一种称为适应性实验的方法更快地将美国材料纳入其混合物中。

> 提出高潜力候选材料：人工智能建议最有可能满足目标规格的新混合物，并可以比较美国制造和外国材料之间的性能

美国22%的水泥依赖进口

> 到 2024 年，美国 34 个州的 99 家工厂将生产波特兰水泥和混合水泥，其中以德克萨斯州、密苏里州、加利福尼亚州和佛罗里达州为首。尽管如此，仍然存在严重的进口依赖。净进口占总消费的22%，主要来源国是土耳其（32%）、加拿大（22%）和越南（10%）。去年美国的水泥出口量可以忽略不计。

https://www.constructconnect.com/construction-economic-news/...。

我认为这不是出于国家安全原因，可能更多的是为了帮助国内产业应对关税。我希望 Meta 利用他们与政府的广泛关系。

> Meta’s AI for concrete model can help suppliers more quickly incorporate U.S. materials into their mixes through an approach called adaptive experimentation.

> Proposes high-potential candidates: The AI suggests new mixes most likely to meet target specifications and can compare performance between U.S.-made and foreign materials

US imports 22% of its cement

> In 2024, Portland and blended cement were produced in 99 plants in 34 U.S. states, led by Texas, Missouri, California, and Florida. Nevertheless, there was significant import reliance. Net imports were 22% of total consumption, with the major source countries being Turkey (32%), Canada (22%), and Vietnam (10%). U.S. exports of cement last year were negligible.

https://www.constructconnect.com/construction-economic-news/....

I'm assuming this isn't for national security reasons, probably more to help the domestic industry deal with tariffs. I hope Meta used their extensive connections to the government.

在 HN 中查看
CrazyStat 2026/04/02 04:22
考虑到这个模型已经有好几年的历史了，我认为这篇文章的重点是融入国内材料，因此马克·扎克伯格下次与特朗普会面时可以指出“美国优先”。

这并不是要贬低这项工作——实验设计可以成为巨大的力量倍增器，让现场的人们更容易接触到它是一件很棒的事情。我最喜欢的研究生课程之一是由四名学生组成的实验设计课程，我们花了大约一半的学期为一名化学博士生进行现实生活中的实验设计，该学生正在尝试（在我们参与之前，大部分都失败了）创建具有特定属性的分子过滤器。

Given the model is several years old, I assume this write up with its focus on incorporating domestic materials is so Mark Zuckerberg has something “America First” to point to next time he meets with Trump.

This is not to denigrate the work—experimental design can be a huge force multiplier and making it more accessible to people on the ground is a great thing to do. One of my favorite grad school courses was an experimental design class with four students where we spent about half of the semester doing real life experimental design for a chemistry phd student who was trying (and mostly failing, before we got involved) to create a molecular filter with specific properties.

nerdralph 2026/04/02 03:50
随着时间的推移，混凝土混合物变得越来越复杂。 Flyash 已经存在了一段时间，GU/L 相对较新，似乎凝固得更快，通常需要缓速器。 有许多不同的减水添加剂可供选择。 加气剂往往会降低强度。 添加到混合物中的纤维或钢销可以提高抗裂性。

间歇式工厂将设计混合物，以便可以在现场添加一些水以提高可操作性。 如果不加水，混凝土可能会超出规格。

影响混凝土强度的因素有很多，坍落度测试只是其中之一。

Concrete mixes have become more complicated over time. Flyash has been around for a while, GU/L is relatively new and seems to set faster, often requiring retarders. Many different water-reducing additives are available. Air entraining agents tend to reduce strength. Fibers or steel pins added to the mix can improve crack resistance.

Batch plants will design mixes so some water can be added on site to improve workability. If you don't add water, the concrete will likely exceed spec.

A slump test is only one factor if many that impact concrete strength.

I had to double check that this wasn't an April Fools joke. The GitHub project has commits from 2 weeks ago, so it's not.

在 HN 中查看
sebastianeament 2026/04/02 02:35
谷歌的“智能Cookie”确实也使用了贝叶斯优化技术。有关一些技术细节，请参阅他们的文章： https://static.googleusercontent.com/media/research.google.c...

我们在混凝土方面的工作的不同之处在于，问题是 1）本质上随时间变化，并且 2）多目标。 有关详细信息，请参阅我们的文章： https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.18288

Google's "Smart Cookie" indeed also used techniques from Bayesian Optimization. For some technical detail, see their write-up here: https://static.googleusercontent.com/media/research.google.c...

Our work on concrete here differs in that the problem is both 1) an inherently time-varying, and 2) multi-objective. See our write-up here for details: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.18288

Hasz 2026/04/02 04:35
伙计，我他妈的喜欢混凝土。太酷了。如果您喜欢混凝土，请查看[Tyler Ley 的 youtube 频道](https://www.youtube.com/@TylerLey/videos）。 不管怎样，我很好奇为什么 Meta 会投资这个。毫无疑问，他们使用了大量混凝土，但与高速公路、所有商业建筑等相比，这根本不算什么。我本以为他们会发布类似非常通用的材料优化框架之类的东西来探索空间，但很高兴看到它专门应用于混凝土。

有很多波特兰水泥的替代品，有兴趣看看是否在范围内。混合物的清单也很长并且相当秘密。 UHPC 是一项非常酷的开发，我特别看好去除钢筋并用 FRP 钢筋代替，以限制随着碳化的逐渐进行而最终出现的锈裂。

不管怎样，非常酷并且期待这个框架的混合开发。

man, I fucking love concrete. So cool. If you like concrete, check out [Tyler Ley's youtube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@TylerLey/videos). Anyways, I am curious why Meta is investing in this. They use a lot of concrete no doubt, but it's nothing compared to a highway, all the commercial builds, etc. I would have expected them to release something like a very general materials optimization framework to explore a space, but glad to see it applied specifically to concrete.

There are a lot of alternative cements to portland, interested to see if that is in-scope. The list of admixtures is also very long and also fairly secretive. UHPC is a pretty cool development, and I am especially bullish about removing rebar and replacing it with FRP bar to limit the eventual rust cracking that comes with the gradual march of carbonation.

Anyways, very cool and looking forward to the mix developments that come out of this framework.

anarticle 2026/04/02 05:42
这也让人感觉“我们即将建造一堆数据中心，但我们无法有效地验证我们站点的混凝土质量。”这使他们能够监控变量，并且如果超出规格，我的猜测可能不会支付。

在我的混凝土搅拌机经验中，这只是整个过程的一部分，还有很多其他的错误，比如形式、振动、水、添加剂。我不是在打基础，所以我的经验只是说有很多事情发生。你猜这是第一步吗？

This also feels like "we're about to build a bunch of datacenters and we cannot meaningfully verify the quality of the concrete at our sites." This enables them to monitor the variables and probably not pay if it's out of spec would be my guess.

In my concrete mixer experience that's just one part of the process, lot of other crap goes wrong, forms, vibing, water, additives. I'm not pouring foundations, so my xp is only to say there's a lot going on. Guess it's a first step?

scythe 2026/04/02 03:06
该网站谈论了制造水泥，但只描述了制造混凝土。制造混凝土涉及在受控条件下将水泥和填料与水混合。制造水泥需要将碳酸钙和氧化物与二氧化硅或硅酸钙一起加热，在不低于 1250°C 的温度下（据我们了解）形成阿利特。通常这是用化石燃料完成的，原材料中的任何杂质（受成本限制）都会进入烟道，使水泥厂产生相当大的污染。二氧化碳是几乎不可避免的副产品（CaCO3 + SiO2 >> CaSiO3 + CO2），要么从源头捕获（大多数设施未实施），要么释放。

水泥生产还有很大的改进空间。我不确定如何应用人工智能，但我想我希望的不止于此。如果我们要实现各种改革派不断谈论的基础设施复兴，我们就需要更多的水泥。

南美洲的水泥生产也令人惊讶地落后，考虑到他们拥有材料并且需要道路，这很奇怪。我认为环境问题和大陆对煤炭的厌恶可能有所贡献。

The website talks about making cement, but only describes making concrete. Making concrete involves mixing cement and fillers with water under controlled conditions. Making cement involves heating calcium carbonates and oxides with silicon dioxide or calcium silicate to form alite at a temperature of (so far as we understand) no less than 1250 C. Usually this is done with fossil fuels and any impurities in the raw materials (which are cost-constrained) go up the flue, making cement plants rather polluting. Carbon dioxide is a nearly inevitable byproduct (CaCO3 + SiO2 >> CaSiO3 + CO2) and is either captured at source (not implemented at most facilities) or released.

There is plenty of room for improvement in cement production. I'm not sure exactly how to apply AI to it but I guess I was hoping for more than this. If we are going to have the infrastructure renaissance that keeps being talked up by reformists of various stripes, we need more cement.

South America is also a surprising laggard in cement production, which is odd considering they have the materials and they need the roads. I think that environmental concerns and a continental aversion to coal might contribute.

AngryData 2026/04/02 01:45
天哪，我希望他们对这些实验混合物进行适当的测试，不要相信人工智能决定你应该混合的任何随机垃圾。这正是人工智能绝对可怕的事情，因为它没有逻辑技能或直接经验或能力来测试它。如果你的人工智能编码的东西失败了，你可以再试一次。如果你的价值数百万美元的水泥地基结果低于标准，那么就需要花费数百万美元将其拆除，然后再花费数百万美元重新进行正确的处理，这是最好的情况。另一种情况是人们在公寓楼倒塌时死亡。
Jesus I hope they do proper testing for these experimental mixes and don't trust whatever random garbage AI decides you should mix in. This is exactly the kind of thing AI is absolutely terrible at because it has no logical skills or direct experience or ability to test it. If your AI coded stuff goes belly up, you get to try again. If your multi million dollar cement foundation turns out to be sub-par, thats multi million dollars to tear it out and then millions more to do it again right, and that is a best case scenario. The alternative is people dieing when their apartment building collapses.
在 HN 中查看
sebastianeament 2026/04/02 02:20
我们使用经过学术和行业合作伙伴审查的测试数据训练的高斯流程。我们利用这些预测来推荐用于现场测试的混合物，以加速寻找具有最佳强度-速度-可持续性权衡的混合物。所有数据和预测都经过检验。这篇博文对此进行了更详细的介绍。
We use Gaussian processes trained on vetted test data from academic and industry partners. We use these predictions to recommend mixes for onsite testing to accelerate finding mixtures with optimal strength-speed-sustainability trade-offs. None of the data and predictions go untested. The blog post goes into this in more detail.
